definition: Contemplation that sees experience as not fit to be taken as self or ownership.
notes: Use this record for the active contemplative side of the not-self mark. It should stay tied to de-appropriation and the release from I-and-mine language, not to metaphysical argument. In the three-marks family it completes the progression from impermanence to dissatisfaction to not-self.

example_phrases.notes: Canonical contemplative designation governing the practice family.
